case.title: STANDARD PIPE LINE CO. v. COMMISSIONERS OF INDEX SULPHUR DRAINAGE

opinion.h4: STANDARD PIPE LINE CO. v. COMMISSIONERS OF INDEX SULPHUR DRAINAGE, (1928)
opinion.h2: STANDARD PIPE LINE COMPANY, Inc., et al., petitioners, v. COMMISSIONERS OF INDEX SULPHUR DRAINAGE DISTRICT.
Supreme Court of the United States
opinion.p: Messrs. T. M. Milling, of Shreveport, La., and William H. Arnold, William H. Arnold, Jr., and David C. Arnold, all of Texarkana, Ark., for petitioner.
Mr. Henry Moore, Jr., of Texarkana, Ark., for respondent.

The writ of certiorari (48 S. Ct. 420) is dismissed for the reason that the decree of the state court sought here to be reviewed was based on a non-Federal ground adequate to support it. Bilby v. Stewart, 246 U.S. 255, 257, 38 S. Ct. 264; Farson, Son & Co. v. Bird, 248 U.S. 268, 271, 39 S. Ct. 111.[ Standard Pipe Line Co v. Comm. of Index Sulphur Drainage Dist. (1928) ]
